campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. They also discussed extending a temporary rule (Ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help fund the project.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship fund from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary Ordinance 2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submission for bridge planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community feedback on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council voted to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first reading step, allowing them to officially adopt it at their next meeting instead of delaying it.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for approval
First reading waived to speed up the adoption process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorized the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered routine administrative tasks and a construction project, though the full details of the construction work weren't fully specified in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract on behalf of the city

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming council sessions. They reviewed what items should be added to the April 21st and April 28th meeting agendas, decided which agenda items needed specific time slots to be discussed, and checked legal requirements for any documents that needed special notice to the public.
keyTopics: April 21, 2026 meeting agenda - finalizing items and timing
April 28, 2026 meeting agenda - adding new items and setting time slots
Public records review from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee matters
Legal notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held two sessions to discuss the city's upcoming spending plans and celebrate community contributions. The council reviewed Mountain View's capital improvement projects and budget for the next fiscal year, then recognized the importance of teen involvement and volunteer efforts in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget and capital improvement projects planning
Teen Month proclamation to recognize young people in Mountain View
National Volunteer Week proclamation honoring community volunteers
Study session on city infrastructure and spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These designations highlight the city's commitment to celebrating its diverse communities and ensuring equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Housing equality and fair practices

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows the public how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who try to influence city decisions to register and report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget information and how to access it
New lobbying registration and reporting requirements
Who needs to register as a lobbyist under city rules

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included an important legal notice for residents. If you disagree with a City decision and want to file a lawsuit, you have 90 days from when the decision becomes final to take legal action.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge City decisions in court
Legal procedures for residents disputing City rulings
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and reviewed new project requests from council members. The council also considered changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan updates for 2025-2030
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to council vacancy procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is requesting to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space in exchange for improving the building's historic and earthquake safety features.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development with historic preservation incentives
